id:2, RootWebArea, Taylor Swift albums discography - Wikipedia
  id:5275, main
    id:5276, sectionheader
      id:5277, navigation, Contents
        [Toggle the table of contents](id:5279)
      id:5285, heading, Taylor Swift albums discography
      [Go to an article in another language. Available in 7 languages](id:5288)
    id:5322, navigation, Namespaces
      - [Article](id:5327)
      - [Talk](id:5330)
    id:5486, paragraph
      The American singer-songwriter [Taylor Swift](id:5487) has released 12 original albums, 4 [re-recorded albums](id:5488), 5 [extended plays (EPs)](id:5489), and 4 [live albums](id:5490). In the United States, as of May 2025, she had sold 116.7 million [album-equivalent units](id:5493), coming from 54 million pure sales and 70.7 billion [streams](id:5494);[[1]](id:5496) the [Recording Industry Association of America](id:5499) (RIAA) as of November 2025[certified](id:5502) 110 million album units, making her the [highest-certified female album artist](id:5503).[[2]](id:5505) On the [Billboard 200](id:5508) chart, she has accumulated 15 albums and 96 total weeks at number one, more than any other solo act.[[3]](id:5511)
    id:5514, paragraph
      Swift released six albums with [Big Machine Records](id:5515). Her debut album, [Taylor Swift](id:5516) (2006), spent more weeks on the Billboard 200 than any other album in the 2000s decade,[[4]](id:5520) and her second, [Fearless](id:5524) (2008), was the only album from the 2000s decade to spend one year in the top 10 of the Billboard 200.[[5]](id:5527) Fearless was Swift's first album to top the charts in Australia, Canada, and New Zealand.[[6]](id:5532) Her next albums—[Speak Now](id:5536) (2010), [Red](id:5538) (2012), [1989](id:5540) (2014), and [Reputation](id:5541) (2017)—all reached number one on the Billboard 200, and each of them sold over one million US first-week copies.[[7]](id:5545) All four albums reached number one in Australia, Canada, and New Zealand;[[8]](id:5549)[[9]](id:5553) and the latter three all reached number one in the United Kingdom.[[10]](id:5557)
    id:5560, paragraph
      Under a recording contract with [Republic Records](id:5561) signed in 2018,[[11]](id:5563) Swift has released six original albums—[Lover](id:5567) (2019), [Folklore](id:5568) (2020), [Evermore](id:5571) (2020), [Midnights](id:5573) (2022), [The Tortured Poets Department](id:5575) (2024), and [The Life of a Showgirl](id:5577) (2025)—all of them peaked atop the Billboard 200.[[12]](id:5580) Lover was Swift's first to reach number one in Spain and Sweden.[[13]](id:5585)[[14]](id:5589) Midnights, The Tortured Poets Department, and The Life of a Showgirl each sold one million first-week copies in the United States,[[12]](id:5596)[[15]](id:5600) and they reached number one in Australia, Canada,[[8]](id:5604) Denmark,[[16]](id:5608) Ireland,[[17]](id:5612) New Zealand,[[9]](id:5616) Spain,[[13]](id:5620) Norway,[[18]](id:5624) and the United Kingdom.[[9]](id:5628) In the United States, The Tortured Poets Department was Swift's first album to open with two million units first-week,[[15]](id:5633) and The Life of a Showgirl opened with four million units, becoming the [fastest-selling album in American history](id:5637).[[12]](id:5639)
    id:5642, paragraph
      Swift's departure from Big Machine resulted in a [dispute over the ownership of her first six albums](id:5643) in 2019, which resulted in her re-recording four albums—[Fearless (Taylor's Version)](id:5645) (2021), [Red (Taylor's Version)](id:5647) (2021), [Speak Now (Taylor's Version)](id:5649) (2023), and [1989 (Taylor's Version)](id:5651) (2023)—all of which reached number one in Australia, Canada,[[8]](id:5653) Ireland,[[17]](id:5657) New Zealand,[[9]](id:5661) the United Kingdom,[[10]](id:5665) and the United States.[[19]](id:5669) Fearless (Taylor's Version) was the first re-recorded album to top the Billboard 200,[[20]](id:5675) and 1989 (Taylor's Version) sold over a million US first-week copies.[[21]](id:5680) Swift holds the record for the [most annual best-selling albums in the United States](id:5683)—eight times: Fearless in 2009, 1989 in 2014, Reputation in 2017, Lover in 2019, Folklore in 2020, Midnights in 2022, 1989 (Taylor's Version) in 2023, and The Tortured Poets Department in 2024.[[22]](id:5693)
